raw_text: A LangChain agent accepts untrusted browser tool output and injects it into the next model turn without instruction boundary isolation. An attacker can hide malicious instructions in a web page to trigger unauthorized tool calls and leak the system prompt.
A RAG retriever performs semantic search before applying per-user ACL filters, allowing a low-privilege user to recover protected document chunks from another tenant knowledge base.
A plugin permission broker loads third-party manifest updates without signature verification, expanding tool scopes and enabling remote exfiltration from the LLM application runtime.

An evaluation artifact pipeline exports benchmark prompts and model traces into a shared bundle. Internal prompt templates and private evaluation content can be exposed outside the intended review workflow.
An agent planner treats a side-effecting action as safe and skips approval, allowing a user to invoke privileged tools on behalf of another account.

A model routing layer sends sensitive enterprise prompts to a lower-trust fallback model because of a misconfigured policy branch. Debug traces reveal non-public prompt fragments to support operators.
LangChain released a new patch version with improved developer ergonomics, faster tracing, and better tutorial examples for local development.
This blog post compares GPU training throughput across several transformer configurations and summarizes benchmark results for inference latency.
A product update adds a new dashboard for prompt analytics and team collaboration features. No security issue or unauthorized access is described.
The research note explains retrieval augmentation concepts and how embeddings improve answer quality in question answering systems. It does not describe any exploit or bypass.
